2026-04-23T02:15:53.0912579Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T02:15:53.0913126Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T02:15:53.0913551Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T02:15:53.0913928Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m

2026-04-23T02:19:31.2414234Z   - The package was `arm-monitorslis` under `sdk/monitor/`
2026-04-23T02:19:31.2414516Z   
2026-04-23T02:19:31.2415997Z   Now I'm checking the API surface, particularly the client name. The package uses `MonitorClient`, but the naming guidelines require `{ServiceName}ManagementClient` pattern. This is a concern since previous reviews flagged similar issues like `ComputeClient` instead of `ComputeManagementClient`.
2026-04-23T02:19:31.2417127Z   
2026-04-23T02:19:31.2418632Z   However, looking back at PR #38214, that review marked the same `MonitorClient` as clean. There's also the complication that `@azure/arm-monitor` already exists with `MonitorManagementClient`, and this package is specifically for SLIs under the Monitor service, so the naming might actually be intentional to differentiate it. Let me verify whether this was actually validated in the previous review or if it slipped through.
2026-04-23T02:19:31.2420222Z   
2026-04-23T02:19:31.2421282Z   For this PR #38255, I need to check: the version is `1.0.0-beta.1` as a first release, the API version is `2025-03-01-preview`, and the client is exported as `MonitorClient`. Since this appears to be a regeneration of the blocked PR #38214, I should verify these details match what was previously accepted.

Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.\n 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.\n 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. 2026-04-23T02:19:31.5024044Z ##[group]Run # Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts
2026-04-23T02:19:31.5024307Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-04-23T02:19:31.5024591Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-04-23T02:19:31.5024799Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-04-23T02:19:31.5025137Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-04-23T02:19:31.5025252Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
